title: Documentation for podman build inconsistent with actual behaviour
body:
### Issue Description

Interrupted builds may leave external "buildah" containers behind.

This is documented on the podman-build manual:
```$ man podman-build```

>        NOTE:  podman  build uses code sourced from the Buildah project to build container images.  This Buildah code creates Buildah containers for the RUN options in container storage. In certain situations, when the podman build
>        crashes or users kill the podman build process, these external containers can be left in container storage. Use the podman ps --all --storage command to see these containers. External containers can be removed with the pod‐
>        man rm --storage command.

However, the `podman rm --storage` command does not work with these external containers, it seems you need to use the `podman rm --force` command (see also #8735)

It also seems that the `podman ps --external` command is more appropriate than `podman ps --all --storage`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create incomplete container by interrupting a build:
```
$ printf "FROM quay.io/libpod/testimage:20200929\nRUN sleep 30\n"| timeout -v 5 podman build -t thiswillneverexist -
STEP 1/2: FROM quay.io/libpod/testimage:20200929
STEP 2/2: RUN sleep 30
timeout: sending signal TERM to command ‘podman’
```
2. List the container:
```
$ podman ps --external
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                              COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
cf3b1cfb40d1  quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018  buildah     25 seconds ago  Storage                 testimage-working-container
```
NB: the `podman ps --all --storage` command documented on the podman-build manual seems to give the same results, but the `--storage` option is not documented on the podman-ps manual and `--external` seems to be the right one

3. Try to remove the container with the `--storage` option:
```
$ podman rm --storage cf3b1cfb40d1
Error: container "cf3b1cfb40d1" is mounted and cannot be removed without using force: container state improper
```
4. Remove the container with the `--force` option:
```
$ podman rm --force cf3b1cfb40d1
cf3b1cfb40d1
```

### Describe the results you received

The podman-build manual says to use the `podman rm --storage` and `podman ps --storage` commands.

### Describe the results you expected

The podman-build manual should instead refer to the `podman rm --force` and `podman ps --external` commands.

### podman info output

```yaml
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.7.2
API Version:  4.7.2
Go Version:   go1.20.10
Built:        Tue Oct 31 15:30:11 2023
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Operating system: Fedora 38
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body:
I have added #20777 as a fix for this. I hope this is the correct change and welcome any comments.

I also confirmed the behaviour of the four commands for myself just as a sanity check.
Any thoughts on the comments from @rhatdan on the linked PR?
From what I see, the `--force` option is required in the case of an interrupted build, even if the container is not really "running".
If the container storage is mounted, CS considers it running.

[Podman-build documents how to list external containers.](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-build.1.md.in#L43-L48)

[Podman-rm documents how to use `--force` in this case.](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-rm.1.md.in#L31-L37)
